summary: Iteration 13: Tested Open in Gmail feature. Found and fixed critical bug: EmailLogCreate Pydantic model missing 'message_type' field causing POST /api/emails to return 500. After fix, all 6 backend tests pass (100%). Frontend flow fully verified: button visibility, disabled state, Gmail URL format, API calls (emails + my-colleges track + status PATCH), success confirmation banner.
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: EmailLogCreate Pydantic model was missing 'message_type: Optional[str] = ""' field. Frontend's openInGmail() sends message_type in the payload but backend model didn't accept it, causing AttributeError and 500 on POST /api/emails.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 13: Open in Gmail fully working. Bug fixed: EmailLogCreate model now has message_type field. All 3 API calls fire correctly: POST /api/emails (direction=sent), POST /api/my-colleges (auto-track, 400 if already tracked), PATCH /api/my-colleges/{id}/status (contacted + follow_up_date=today+7). Success banner shows with college name. Gmail URL format: mail.google.com/?view=cm&fs=1&su=...&body=...
verified_features: Open in Gmail button hidden when no draft - PASS
Button visible and enabled when college + draft both set - PASS
Button disabled when no college selected - PASS (disabled attr set)
Gmail URL constructed correctly (mail.google.com, view=cm, su=, body=) - PASS
PATCH /api/my-colleges/{id}/status called with contacted + follow_up_date - PASS
College appears in dashboard alerts upcoming_followups - PASS
my-colleges entry has status=contacted and correct follow_up_date - PASS
rca_of_issue: Root cause: EmailLogCreate Pydantic model at server.py:142 did not include 'message_type' field. The openInGmail() frontend function added message_type to its POST payload (server.py:131). FastAPI's Pydantic validation accepted the body (extra fields ignored by default) but the doc construction at line 899 referenced 'data.message_type' which threw AttributeError since the field wasn't declared. Fix: added 'message_type: Optional[str] = ""' to EmailLogCreate.
